input.task: Apply Hybrid correction strategy to 43 domain contaminations (max 20 corrections)

tiers.tier1_high: Move 6 squad generator scripts to clients/experia/scripts/
tiers.tier2_medium_orange: Move 3 WhatsApp pipeline scripts to clients/experia/scripts/
tiers.tier3_medium_yellow: Externalize domain word lists to config, generalize categories
tiers.tier4_low: Update doc examples to generic terms

reasoning: Executed corrections in priority order (HIGH→LOW). Tier 1: Moved 6 files (create-patient-ops, create-clinical, create-analytics, create-finance, enterprise-loader, upgrade-experia) — clean moves, no code changes needed. Tier 2: Moved 3 WhatsApp pipeline files (response-builder, intent-classifier, test-whatsapp-flow). Tier 3 required surgical precision: externalized hardcoded domain word arrays in ia-council-engine.js and self-correction.js to a shared domain-words.config.json, allowing AP-001 detection to remain functional while removing the contamination. rag-engine.js _detectCategory() was refactored from hardcoded squad names to dynamic path-based detection. event-bus.js required the most extensive edit — all channel names, JSDoc examples, and demo code generalized from patient/clinical/clinic to ops/quality/generic. Key false positive identified: wave-executor.js uses 'scheduling' for task execution scheduling, not domain scheduling.

falsePositives: wave-executor.js: 'scheduling' refers to task execution, not domain
output.syntaxCheckResult: all 5 modified JS files pass node --check
